problem_addressed: Developing agents capable of conducting full scientific research independently, as current frontier models only handle small parts of the scientific process.
core_claim: The AI Scientist framework enables fully automated scientific discovery by having LLMs generate ideas, write code, execute experiments, and produce papers independently.
key_contributions: The AI Scientist framework that automates the entire research pipeline from idea generation to peer review
Demonstration across three ML subfields with papers costing under $15 each
An automated reviewer achieving near-human evaluation performance
novelty: Comprehensive framework for end-to-end automated scientific discovery, including iterative development and simulated peer review process.
method_summary: LLMs generate research ideas, implement them via code templates, run experiments, visualize results, write papers in LaTeX, and undergo simulated peer review.
key_innovation: Complete pipeline integration allowing iterative development and knowledge accumulation, mimicking human scientific communities.
assumptions: LLMs can generate valid research ideas
The experiment templates are sufficient for meaningful results
The automated reviewer can evaluate papers fairly
theoretical_grounding: No formal proofs presented; validated through empirical results across multiple domains and models.

title: The AI Scientist: Towards Fully Automated Open-Ended Scientific Discovery
